actionable_feedback: Open attempts/baseline/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] in Excel (or another .xlsx-capable viewer) and confirm that the cited ranges (Inputs!A5:F7, Inputs!A11:E13, Model!A4:V27, Summary!A10:E18) contain the values and formulas asserted in expert_memo.md.

Verify that each computed value used in the memo (capacities, demands, shortfalls, remediation effects, remediation total costs, and break-even penalty calculations) is produced by live formulas (not pasted constants). If any values are pasted, convert them to formula-driven cells that reference source input cells.

Recompute the R1/R2/R3 O2 break-even penalty values from the workbook formulas and reconcile the small rounding discrepancy noted for R3; either correct the formula precision or document the rounding method used. Store break-even calculations in explicit workbook cells and reference them in the memo.
Add explicit provenance links in the workbook: a provenance column or direct cell hyperlinks that identify the exact Input cell(s) feeding each derived row (e.g., 'Inputs!B6' or 'R2.capacity_units').

Add clarity formulas in the spreadsheet: computed remediation-cost totals that reference remediation cost cells (no hard-coded constants), a 'feasible' boolean column for budget checks, and a numeric 'slack' column showing surplus/deficit vs max_budget.

Complete the truncated line and ending of expert_memo.md. Include exact cell coordinates (sheet and cell, e.g., Summary!B12) for every sensitivity table entry and for the numbers called out in the memo (especially the break-even values).

After workbook verification and memo updates, attach a short verification note or screenshot to the memo (or include a short appendix) that documents the inspection and confirms formulas matched the memo claims. Then re-run the grader/verifier and update the semantic/final score or add an explicit verification statement.
Ensure any formatting/rounding rules are documented in the memo (e.g., rounding to 2 decimals, currency formatting) so graders understand why displayed numbers may differ from raw formula values.
